(NOT   .  but she ought to) There are more flowers than there used to be. (NOT   . . . than there used to.) Be and have (used for possession) are not usually dropped.

‘ Somebody ought to clean up the bathroom.’ ’ I'll ask Jack to.' Sorry I shouted al you. I didn't mean to.

I don't dance much  now, but I used to a lot.
